Echo (Class I Spirit).summary: A psychic recording of a traumatic death, repeating endlessly. Cannot be negotiated with.
Echo (Class I Spirit).lore: Not truly a spirit, but an imprint. Requires a witness to speak the truth of the event to fade.

Anchor (Class II Spirit).summary: A spirit bound to an object, location, or person by unresolved emotion. Possesses will and memory.
Anchor (Class II Spirit).lore: Can be negotiated with to fulfill unfinished business.

Poltergeist (Class IV Spirit).summary: A corrupted Anchor, driven by rage or hunger. A wound that has learned to strike back.
locations: The Plague House of Port Shed
Poltergeist (Class IV Spirit).lore: Progresses through stages from The Knock to The Mask (possession).

Ghoul (Starved Anchor).summary: An Anchor that has begun consuming life force to sustain itself. Retains intelligence.
Ghoul (Starved Anchor).lore: Can be negotiated with if fed; fire and silver are key weaknesses.

Linn Sea-Wolf (Lycanthrope).summary: A lycanthrope that transforms into a massive seal or walrus. Solitary and melancholic.
Linn Sea-Wolf (Lycanthrope).lore: Transforms during the first winter storm after a kill. Vulnerable to silver.

Ykrul Sky-Hound (Lycanthrope).summary: A hawk-headed hunter that transforms during the day to hunt oath-breakers.
Ykrul Sky-Hound (Lycanthrope).lore: Triggered by the sight of blood on fresh snow. Hunts for justice, not flesh.

Valewood Root-Walker (Lycanthrope).summary: Transforms into a tree, immobile but all-seeing. Guardians, not predators.
Valewood Root-Walker (Lycanthrope).lore: Triggered by a witnessed promise of hospitality.

Aeler Stone-Hide (Lycanthrope).summary: Massive, slow lycanthrope that transforms only underground. Nearly invulnerable.
Aeler Stone-Hide (Lycanthrope).lore: Cannot transform in sunlight. Silver weapon to the heart or brain.

Baron Quentin Asselin (Vampire).summary: The first vampire and one of the Thirteen. Disillusioned and melancholic.
locations: A secluded estate in a remote valley
Baron Quentin Asselin (Vampire).lore: Converts guests or drives them mad. Never accept his wine.

Sister Maris (Vampire).summary: One of the Thirteen. Runs a children's hospice where the children never leave.
Sister Maris (Vampire).lore: The Everflame has tried to destroy her hospice four times, but the children defend her.

The Sparrow (Vampire).summary: One of the Thirteen. Kills only the wicked, or so he claims.
The Sparrow (Vampire).lore: Cannot look at mirrors. Has saved Saikou Ira's life twice.

Linn Draugr (Vampire).summary: Vampires that breathe underwater, trapped by sea-salt circles.
Linn Draugr (Vampire).lore: A lesser bloodline of the Thirteen.

Aeler Stone-Sleepers (Vampire).summary: Vampires that drink breath, awakened by bronze bells at dawn.

Ykrul Bone-Eaters (Vampire).summary: Vampires that turn to dust when killed but rise if their name is spoken.
Ykrul Bone-Eaters (Vampire).lore: Erase the name to destroy them permanently.

Vhasian Candle-Wights (Vampire).summary: Vampires that extinguish flames through windows. A lit candle at the threshold bars them.

Lethai-ar Silk-Drinkers (Vampire).summary: Vampires that feed on desire. Vulnerable to vows witnessed by a third party.
locations: The Court Painter of Mbaro's domain

The Oath-Keeper (Possessor Variant).summary: A spirit of unpaid obligation that wears the face of the person you failed.
The Oath-Keeper (Possessor Variant).lore: Manifests from your own memory. It accuses, does not attack. Can possess a host.

The Counting Wraith.summary: A ghost of an error that appears when someone miscounts nine steps, cups, or breaths.
The Counting Wraith.lore: Can only be resolved by a correct count. Takes memories as payment for errors.

The Weeping Shade.summary: A ghost bound by a secret promise of silence, unable to speak its unfinished business.
The Weeping Shade.lore: Can only be resolved by someone speaking the secret aloud in the presence of a witness.

The Unwitnessed Door.summary: A threshold entity that appears as a gray door where no door stood before.
The Unwitnessed Door.lore: Opens to a room with two mirrors. Choosing one transforms the character.

The Deep Drake (Pressure that Thinks).summary: A mythic creature dwelling in the deepest galleries beneath Khaz-Vurim.
locations: The Heart of the Ninth Gallery
The Deep Drake (Pressure that Thinks).lore: A wyrm, child of Nidhoggr. It invites intruders to count their breaths, then consumes them.

Whisperer (Imp - Rank I Demon).summary: A demon of small bargains, offering power through contracts witnessed by no one.
Whisperer (Imp - Rank I Demon).lore: A contract sealed without a third party is a hole shaped like your own hand.

Ravager (Fury - Rank II Demon).summary: A demon summoned by violence or mass death. It does not speak; it erases.
Ravager (Fury - Rank II Demon).lore: Banishment requires overwhelming witness—three or more souls refusing to look away.

Possessor (Marquis - Rank III Demon).summary: A demon that possesses a host, slowly eroding their identity.
locations: Where a soul has said 'yes' to something they should have refused
Possessor (Marquis - Rank III Demon).lore: Exorcism requires a witness from the host's faith and the demon's true name.

Parasite (Duke - Rank IV Demon).summary: A demon that embodies an emotion: fear, grief, or ambition, starving the body.
Parasite (Duke - Rank IV Demon).lore: Countered by rigorous emotional discipline and sensory deprivation.

Trow (Shadow Bargainer).summary: Enigmatic fey who keep to thresholds and almost-there spaces. Bound by courtesy.
locations: The space between departure and arrival
Trow (Shadow Bargainer).lore: Hint at an unnamed patron like the Gray Benefactor. Payment must be in honest memory.

The Neighbors.summary: The forest's awareness of intruders, manifesting as silence and watching.
The Neighbors.lore: Manifest as an Oath-Keeper when a taboo is broken.

Hazel Queen's Emissary.summary: A fae courtier wearing leaves and law, bound by the Green Court's rules.
locations: Where the Hazel Queen's influence holds
Hazel Queen's Emissary.lore: Cannot lie but may omit. Any gift accepted creates a debt.

Lady of Thorns.summary: Sovereign of a hawthorn court. She never lies; she simply doesn't need to.
Lady of Thorns.lore: Cannot be fought or bargained with on unequal terms. Offer a gift of equal value.

Stone Giant.summary: A giant of living rock, found in mountain passes and under-vaults.
Stone Giant.lore: Pacified by a correct count of eight. Count to nine, and it will become hostile.

Frost Giant.summary: A giant wrapped in permafrost, appearing during the deepest winters.
Frost Giant.lore: Driven back by a true fire, a hearth-fire that has burned for nine generations.

Dream Giant.summary: A giant that appears in thin places and dreams, asking questions.
Dream Giant.lore: Banished by answering its question truthfully or by refusing to answer at all.

Debt-Ogre.summary: An ogre drawn to broken promises, taking the memory of the broken promise.
Debt-Ogre.lore: Can be satisfied by speaking the broken promise aloud, even if you don't remember it.

Flesh-Ogre.summary: A huge, bloated ogre drawn to battlefields, scavenging the dead and living.
Flesh-Ogre.lore: Driven off by fire or a more appealing offering.

Stone-Ogre.summary: An ogre covered in rocky hide, guarding places where the earth has been wounded.
Stone-Ogre.lore: Pacified by repairing the wound to the land.

Crater Oni.summary: A vast oni sleeping beneath a caldera, dreaming of waking.
Crater Oni.lore: Sealed by performing the old rites: a pilgrimage, a true name spoken at midnight.

River Oni.summary: A clever oni dwelling in deep pools, offering bargains to the lost and desperate.
River Oni.lore: Driven off by a true name.

Dream Oni.summary: An oni that appears in dreams, offering bargains and taking memories.
Dream Oni.lore: Banished by speaking a truth never told or by waking.

Goblin Scavenger.summary: Common pests in the Crown Spread, known for their greed and cowardice.
Goblin Scavenger.lore: Hunger's fingers. Can be bribed, threatened, or tricked.

Tunnel-Goblin.summary: Pale, blind, and skittering goblins adapted to the darkness of the deep ways.
Tunnel-Goblin.lore: Navigate by echolocation. Weak to bright light.

Ember-Goblin.summary: Goblins touched by the fires of Zahk'Kozahn, carrying the heat of Malachai's forge.
Ember-Goblin.lore: Explode in a burst of ash and heat when killed. Weak to water.

Sea-Goblin.summary: Webbed-fingered goblins that patrol the tide-lines, scavenging what the sea brings.

Star-Goblin.summary: Dust-gray goblins living in the high passes, their eyes reflecting constellations.
Star-Goblin.lore: Star-touched, leaving behind a glowing crystal shard when killed.

Hobgoblin.summary: Taller, disciplined goblinoids acting as the Hunger's enforcers.
Hobgoblin.lore: Hunger's fist. Respect strength and order. Can be reasoned with from a position of strength.

Bugbear.summary: Large, silent hunters of the goblinoid kind, patient and relentless.
Bugbear.lore: Hunger's stalker. Cannot be bargained with; can only be distracted or out-hunted.

Warg.summary: Lean, wolf-like predators that patrol boundary edges, drawn to frayed balance.
Warg.lore: Hunger's reminder. Respond to imbalance, not aggression.

Earth-Shark.summary: A burrowing predator with a dorsal ridge like a plowshare.
Earth-Shark.lore: Something older that learned hunger from the sea and brought it to land.

Dust Hulk.summary: A wind-packed silhouette that rises from dunes, a memory given weight by thirst.
Dust Hulk.lore: Cannot cross running water. Spill water on its shadow to dissolve it.

Bone Kite.summary: A carrion bird with rune-etched pinions, feeding on the dying.
Bone Kite.lore: Can see the weight of unfulfilled promises. Accepts name-stakes as barter.

Salt Screamer.summary: A pale eel in dry salt pans with a voice like a flute. Its scream is a question.
Salt Screamer.lore: Asks 'What is your name?' Answering truthfully grants a boon; lying advances its patience.

Phase Panther.summary: A shadow-striped cat that steps between gusts of wind.
Phase Panther.lore: Cannot phase through mirrored water. A silver mirror fascinates it.

Lichen-Bear.summary: A moss-matted ursine with rock-teeth, carrying the memory of a lost forest.
Lichen-Bear.lore: Smoke of juniper calms it; salt enrages it. Burn the moss to kill it.

Bramble Knight.summary: An empty helm grown through with thorn, a hedge that learned to walk.
Bramble Knight.lore: Cannot cross a line of salt. Accepts a hedge-blessing as safe passage.

Reef Serpent.summary: A kelp-maned constrictor nesting in pilings, a rope that forgot it was tied.
Reef Serpent.lore: Beat a drum in a rhythm it doesn't recognize to confuse it.

Grave-Eel.summary: An eel that burrows through barrow loam and tastes names.
Grave-Eel.lore: A question that learned to wiggle. Feed it a false name to confuse it.

Vein-Serpent.summary: A serpent of polished copper and malachite, swimming through stone.
Vein-Serpent.lore: The mountain's blood given form. Can be negotiated with to maintain the stone.

Wind Drake.summary: A rib-thin sky serpent that rides storm fronts.
Wind Drake.lore: Whistle back the storm to calm it. Ground it with a copper-weighted net.

Rust Moth.summary: A swarm of iron-scaled moths that taste memory and rust metal.
Rust Moth.lore: Attracted to old keys. Repelled by a copper bell.

Lantern-Eye.summary: A floating orb of watchlight, jealous of secrets.
Lantern-Eye.lore: The dungeon's attention made visible. Offer a whispered truth to pacify it.

Skeleton Knight.summary: Ancient guardians of forgotten tombs, often found in barrows.
Skeleton Knight.lore: Believed to be the remains of a fallen order of paladins.

Thorn Dryad.summary: Fey guardians that are fiercely protective of old forests.
Thorn Dryad.lore: Connected to the Green Court and the Queen of Thorns.

Slavering Hound.summary: Used by the Salt Prince's huntsmen to track fugitives.

Cultist Fanatic.summary: Devotees of the Drowned God, eager to die for their faith.
Cultist Fanatic.lore: Eager to die for their faith.

Shadow Wraith.summary: Souls twisted by despair, lingering in the place of their death.
